round1: Round 1 is done, and if you picked Vancouver, Boston, Pittsburgh, or Chicago, congratulations, you're officially a certified hockey expert... from 2011. While Marc and Steph are laughing all the way to the bank with their upset picks, the rest of you are left with brackets resembling a dumpster fire.
round2: Round 2 is done, and it seems most of you are better at guessing lottery numbers than hockey outcomes. Marc, nice work actually getting one right with the Rangers, but the rest of you who picked Nashville, Washington, or Philly can officially stop watching. Derrick, congrats on going 0-for-4, a truly impressive feat of futility.
round3: Round 3 is in the books, and while everyone correctly picked the Kings, only Derrick and Nathan bothered to get the games right. The rest of you who thought the Rangers had a chance against the Devils clearly need your eyes checked.
round4: Round 4 wraps up with LA taking it in 6. Congrats to Jaclyn and Nathan for actually watching the games, unlike everyone else who picked LA but missed the mark, or Derrick, who apparently still thinks the Devils are a thing.
overall: Alright, another NHL season wraps up, and what a dismal showing for most in the pool, with Jaclyn and newcomer Nathan somehow stumbling their way to a co-championship with a paltry 15 points each. Jaclyn, after cratering to last place last year, pulled off a redemption arc, proving that sometimes just being less wrong than everyone else is enough to win this thing. Meanwhile, last year's reigning champion, Marc, performed an incredible face-plant, going from king of the hill to sharing the basement with Nickall, who continues his impressive 17-year streak of never winning, and Stephanie, who's now a six-time veteran of the last-place club. Ryan also joined the illustrious bottom tier, while Derrick merely existed in the middle with his five points, making this one of the lower-scoring playoff pools in recent memory.
